hypothesis: When extending the GraphSAGE framework to three-or-more component gas mixtures (e.g., Ar/O₂/CF₄), assigning edge features that encode pairwise reaction rate constants or cross-section data derived from the individual single-gas PBMs—rather than treating all edges as unweighted—will yield significantly higher prediction accuracy (R² improvement >0.05) compared to the unweighted complete-graph formulation, because explicitly encoding known pairwise chemical interaction strengths into the graph topology will allow the GNN aggregation step to prioritize dominant inter-species coupling pathways that are otherwise indistinguishable from weaker or negligible interactions.
